en: What is the _average_ number of employees for the given year? Approximate if necessary.

Any unexpected injury or incident that occurs at work or commuting to and from work is classified as a work accident. Occupational diseases/illnesses are those incidents that occur due to the scope of the work being carried out or due to the hazards present within the work environment.
Did your company experience any work accidents or occupational diseases/illnesses for the given year?
What is the total number of hours worked?
Work accidents or occupational diseases that result in an employee death.
How many fatalities were there for the given year?
Please indicate the number of injuries that resulted in the employee not being able to attend work for <u>more</u> than 15 consecutive days.
Number of injuries that are classified as _over-fifteen-day_ injuries?
Please indicate the number of injuries that resulted in the employee not being able to attend work but for <u>less</u> than 15 consecutive days.
Number of injuries that are classified as _under-fifteen-day_ injuries?
Those incidents which were <u>not</u> classified above but medical treatment was administered to the injured individual. Incidents where only first aid was administered should not be counted.
Number of injuries where the employee did not miss any days of work?

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.
